[Home](./index.md) &gt; [sh-ast-analyze](./sh-ast-analyze.md) &gt; [WordResolutionReason](./sh-ast-analyze.wordresolutionreason.md)

## WordResolutionReason type

Why a [resolveWord()](./sh-ast-analyze.resolveword.md) result is `static: false`<!-- -->: the word's exact text cannot be determined from source alone.

- `'expansion'` ‚Äî the word contains a `ParamExp` (`$x`<!-- -->, `${x}`<!-- -->), a `CmdSubst` (command substitution, either `$(...)` or backtick form), an `ArithmExp` (`$((expr))`<!-- -->), or a `ProcSubst` (`<(cmd)`<!-- -->/`>(cmd)`<!-- -->) part: its value depends on program state (variables, command output, process substitution) that only exists at run time. - `'tilde'` ‚Äî an unquoted, unescaped `~` triggers Bash tilde expansion (Bash Reference Manual ¬ß3.5.2, "Tilde Expansion") at the start of the word, or ‚Äî when `resolveWord`<!-- -->'s `context` option is `'assignment-value'` or omitted ‚Äî immediately after an unquoted `:` anywhere in the word too (¬ß3.5.2 also documents this for assignment-statement values, e.g. `PATH=/foo:~/bar`<!-- -->). Either way: statically shaped, but its value depends on the filesystem/environment (`$HOME`<!-- -->, NSS user database lookups) at run time. See `resolveWord`<!-- -->'s `context` parameter doc for the full command-argument-vs-assignment-value distinction. - `'glob'` ‚Äî the word contains an unquoted, unescaped glob metacharacter (`*`<!-- -->/`?`<!-- -->), an unquoted bracket expression (`[...]` ‚Äî an unquoted `[` with a later unquoted `]` in the same word, POSIX 2.13.1 / Bash Reference Manual ¬ß3.5.8.1, "Pattern Matching"), or an `ExtGlob` part (Bash Reference Manual ¬ß3.5.8, "Filename Expansion"): its value depends on which filesystem entries exist at run time. - `'brace'` ‚Äî the word contains a `BraceExp` part (`{a,b}`<!-- -->/`{1..10}`<!-- -->, Bash Reference Manual ¬ß3.5.1, "Brace Expansion"). mvdan/sh v3.13.1 only ever produces this node type when its caller applies `syntax.SplitBraces` to the parsed tree; this bridge's shim does not call it, so today a brace expression like `{a,b}` reaches [resolveWord()](./sh-ast-analyze.resolveword.md) as an ordinary `Lit` (indistinguishable, in the AST this bridge produces, from literal `{`<!-- -->/`,`<!-- -->/`}` characters) rather than as a `BraceExp` part. This reason is handled here for forward-compatibility with that node type and is not reachable through [resolveWord()](./sh-ast-analyze.resolveword.md) today. - `'locale'` ‚Äî the word contains a `$"..."` locale-translated string (Bash Reference Manual ¬ß3.1.2.5, "Locale-Specific Translation"): its value is looked up in the current locale's translation catalog at run time via `gettext`<!-- -->, regardless of whether its contents also contain an expansion. `'locale'` takes precedence over any `'expansion'` a `$"..."` word's \*contents\* would otherwise report ‚Äî the `DblQuoted` node's own `dollar` flag is checked before its children are visited at all, so e.g. `$"hi $x"` reports `'locale'`<!-- -->, never `'expansion'`<!-- -->. - `'unsupported'` ‚Äî the word contains a `$'...'` (ANSI-C quoted) `\u`<!-- -->/`\U` escape whose value has no corresponding Unicode character (greater than `U+10FFFF`<!-- -->, e.g. `$'\UFFFFFFFF'`<!-- -->): real bash's output for this case is an undocumented, non-UTF-8 byte sequence with no exact representation as a JavaScript string (see `ansi-c-escapes.ts`<!-- -->'s `MAX_UNICODE_CODE_POINT` doc comment for the empirical detail) ‚Äî this package declines to claim a `static: true` text bash never actually produces.

This union may grow in a \*\*minor\*\* release ‚Äî new mvdan/sh word-part node types, or new statically-shaped-but-dynamic categories, can add new reason strings without that being a breaking change. It is deliberately not sealed against extension elsewhere in the codebase. A reason string this version of the package doesn't yet know about only ever accompanies `static: false`<!-- -->, never changes the meaning of `static: true`<!-- -->, so runtime consumers that only branch on `.static` are safe by construction across such additions; an exhaustive compile-time `switch` over `.reason` should still include a `default` case to stay forward-compatible.

**Signature:**

```typescript
export type WordResolutionReason = 'expansion' | 'tilde' | 'glob' | 'brace' | 'locale' | 'unsupported';
```
